reviews.review_msg: Dr. Yellowhorn makes his TA do all the work for his course. He is not accessible via email, despite the course being distance-ed. The workload for the 100-level course is obscene and up to the final paper, I was getting an A+. He gave me a low mark despite performing well for the entirety of the course. He also gives no feedback for his grade..

The workload for this class was beyond what i expected for a 100 level class and took up more time than my 400 level BUS courses. Weekly workbook assignments took forever to finish due to the absurd amount of readings and videos, some of which are over 2 hours. The book review had no grading criteria and left you confused with the grade you got.

Dr. Yellowhorn is fine but he takes forever to grade his papers. I took this course distance-ed so I haven't had any contact with him throughout the course, not sure how much he even creates within it. The TA has marked and made all announcements so far, he is only grading our final paper. It's been almost three weeks and no mark yet.. seriously?

Dr. Yellowhorn makes his TA do all the work for his course. He is not accessible via email, despite the course being distance-ed. The workload for the 100-level course is obscene and up to the final paper, I was getting an A+. He gave me a low mark despite performing well for the entirety of the course. He also gives no feedback for his grade..

The workload for this class was beyond what i expected for a 100 level class and took up more time than my 400 level BUS courses. Weekly workbook assignments took forever to finish due to the absurd amount of readings and videos, some of which are over 2 hours. The book review had no grading criteria and left you confused with the grade you got.

Dr. Yellowhorn is fine but he takes forever to grade his papers. I took this course distance-ed so I haven't had any contact with him throughout the course, not sure how much he even creates within it. The TA has marked and made all announcements so far, he is only grading our final paper. It's been almost three weeks and no mark yet.. seriously?

Dr. Yellowhorn makes his TA do all the work for his course. He is not accessible via email, despite the course being distance-ed. The workload for the 100-level course is obscene and up to the final paper, I was getting an A+. He gave me a low mark despite performing well for the entirety of the course. He also gives no feedback for his grade..

The workload for this class was beyond what i expected for a 100 level class and took up more time than my 400 level BUS courses. Weekly workbook assignments took forever to finish due to the absurd amount of readings and videos, some of which are over 2 hours. The book review had no grading criteria and left you confused with the grade you got.

Dr. Yellowhorn is fine but he takes forever to grade his papers. I took this course distance-ed so I haven't had any contact with him throughout the course, not sure how much he even creates within it. The TA has marked and made all announcements so far, he is only grading our final paper. It's been almost three weeks and no mark yet.. seriously?

The amount of work required is fair. Lots of readings but fulfilling through the end. Put some effort and you will be doing well. Lectures were pretty enjoyable and so were tutorials. Workload isn't as heavy so you could take this a as part or full time student
Lectures were content heavy, and pretty boring. More interesting content could've been chosen for class material and final project. Project not really connected to lectures.

Very kind lecturer. The topic wasn't one I was particularly interested in, nor would I have taken if not mandatory for business students, but her lectures were engaging and she's very passionate and knowledgeable. One complaint I have was the lack of timely feedback, but that was more to do with her TA's and she was reachable to rectify the issue.

50% of course was 2 online open book midterms
TAs marked the discussion posts harshly and inconsistently

Review of the online section of the class. Not sure how she teaches the in person section. Course is not even taught by her, it is prerecorded lectures from another prof (Joyce). Like below review stated, 50% of grade in 5 quizzes. Each quiz requires crazy memorization based on around 4-5 readings, videos, lectures. Not a GPA booster as many claim.

This class is ridiculous, more about memorization than content. 50% of your grade is MC quizzes. Professor is unfamiliar with content as it is an old class from another professor. Unresponsive to messages and does not appreciate questions. TAs were great. Has potential to be a great class with a little effort from prof. Do not recommend.

Don't take any course with her. Not only was the grading incredibly unclear, but unsupportive and unhelpful feedback was given with little to no explanation. Weekly emails were harsh, passive- aggressive, and discouraging. Very little guidance on how to succeed in this course and incredibly hurtful behaviour from the professor. 0/10.

I would caution students before taking INDG101 with Chelsey Armstrong. Expectations are unclear and overly high for an intro class, with harsh grading and little guidance on how to succeed. Weekly emails felt discouraging, and strict AI rules led to unfair accusations without clear evidence. Overall, the environment felt stressful than supportive.
DO NOT HARM YOUR GPA !!!! This class is harder than engineering upper devision courses because instead of professor helping you to get good she tries to make it harder for you !!!

Mt and final did not have clear guidelines and even the TA's were confused about the marking scheme. Unapproachable about marking too. Class and tutorials were a joke. There were in class quizzes every week that made you keep up but other than that, going to lecture was not worth it.
She demands too much for this type of course. Shes more focused on grammar and writing structure than the actual content itself. Do not fill your Indigenous requirement with this professor.

The worst part of this class is having to be there for the quizzes, but they aren't hard-- and they give you a grade boost. Podcast reviews seem informal but do be very formal, and careful with your language. The midterm and final were easy although not worth much, (the instructions are laid out clearly for the student). Pretty easy course!

Armstrong was a super interesting prof and kept classes interesting even when topics seemed boring at times with her tone. Readings were mostly fine and final exam was very fair. Midterm definitely is way harder and marks drop through that though it increases back up after. TA's were great and was enjoyable overall.

She was very straightforward with what she wanted for the midterm and final, which was nice. Unfortunately, this wasn't nearly as helpful as it should have been as the grading for the midterm was extremely harsh and subjective. The material itself was really great though, and class does a great job at incorporating important topics into one course

We have quizzes every morning first thing in the morning- my class was at 8:30am. Each quiz is worth 5% and is locked after 10 minutes, so if you are late by even 5, you get screwed. So much reading too, and you have 1-2 hour long podcasts to listen and review. For the amount of work done, it was barely worth it. Midterm and final were easy though.

Quizzes each lecture that are done in class and are worth 5% each, if you do good in those, you should be set for the class. The midterm and final were ez, you could study for 3ish hours before and do good. Just follow the criteria and don't get caught with AI, she's strict abt that.

weekly quizzes on the lots of readings, but are not difficult if readings are done. the midterm and final was overviewed in much detail before in lecture and in study guide, which is very helpful. found the lectures preachy at times, but was still the most engaging Indigenous studies course ive taken so far.

Chelsey is a top notch professor who has a great way of delivering information so that people can understand it. Her lectures are great. Tests are challenging only if you didn't listen or do any readings.

While there was a decent amount of reading, I think that anyone who actually does them will do fine in this course. A midterm study guide was provided that explicitly stated how to get full marks and practice questions. I personally didn't prefer her lecturing style, but this course wasn't as bad as people make it out to be.

It's not a hard class this was my elective and if you did the readings you can easily get an A people are just mad the grades weren't given to them but it's just a skill issue genuinely the easiest content in a course and she gave us the midterm questions. She's nice, kinda anal about and it's kinda boring

She was very intentional in how she taught Indg 101 while not being Indigenous herself. Lots of readings but she's approachable in class and well organized. People were upset about the midterm grade, but to be honest, she gave a study guide with more or less the same questions as the exam and a step by step breakdown of what she wanted.
Most unfair course, I have ever had in my life time.

Horrible class, took this course to learn about Indigenous studies. She is the worst professor that I ever had in my life, I never tried this hard and get such low mark for any course in my life. Btw I was a Stanford University student, and I can tell you I have never seen such an unfair grader ever in my entire life.

Just had a lecture post midterm grades. Despite push back from students, professor stayed firm in saying "first year grades are supposed to be bad." Being a required business course who many students cannot get into until upper years I'd expect a higher average. 64% is a joke. Straight up lazy making it out of 15 marks. Very, very disappointed.

MT was a joke.Avg was 64%.The weight of each question did not make sense and the TA was having trouble explaining what the prof wanted during our tutorial.Unapproachable.Said students were playing the victim b/c of the MT, but students were just confused about it and she did not go into the MT other than to try and justify the avg.Don't take this
Super disorganized course, subjective grading for midterm, vague syllabus and lack of professionalism

The podcast was nonsense, she asked students to summarise the weekly slide to be the weekly slide is posted right before class same as the due date of the podcast. The student can't summarise it and she uses it to give a low mark. Don't take this course coz no matter what she got a way to give you a low mark.

The midterm is hilarious, the grading was totally a gamble, I bet I will have a better result going to a casino. She was having a great trip herself but not giving up a normal gradingFor whoever wants to take this course, do not take it unless you are ready to give up all other courses and only study this for the whole term.

Midterm marks are like the slots; a complete gamble whether or not you get the point. The answers she was looking for were only mildly related to the questions given. It was implied we could get the answer based on vibes and telepathy. Not to be overdramatic, but her marking parameters discourage critical thinking.
tagged chelsey as hilarious cuz this class is a joke

Just had the first midterm, it was very easy and straight forward. Unfortunately the prof was on a power trip and decided to go on a grading power trip. Her grading overpowered my ability to learn and truly enjoy this course for its intended purpose.
I swear they don't even know what they are grading. Just a coin toss on every question. Would not recommend this class to anyone.

AVOID AT ALL COSTS! Take any other SFU prof because this is seriously a can't win class. Unless you're prepared to spend a 40 hour work week on an elective then save yourself the trouble. Also, disregard the exam questions because whatever they are asking isn't actually what they are grading.

If you're looking for a GPA booster avoid this class and the prof at all costs. During exams she will dock you marks for not being able to read her mind and answer additional parts to the questions. This is no free A so be prepared to put in the hardest work of your entire University career if you take this class.

weekly tests that you HAD to be there for based on the weekly readings. students who came to lectures were definitely rewarded (through quizzes and bonus assignments where if you were in class you were guaranteed the marks). Chelsey was very approachable and really cared for each students success. lots of reading.

Boring class, might've been more engaged if the professor for INDG 101 was Indigenous but she can't control that. Lectures were very preachy without much critical thinking or useful ideas. 3 hour long lectures with one 10 min break. Plenty of homework from readings, podcast reviews, etc. At least she was an easy grader.

Professor Armstrong is very organized, each week you can see what needs to be done and there are weekly quizzes done in lecture worth 45% of your grade. The quizzes are based on the two readings done each week and some lecture material. Although it's a three hour lecture, she makes it very engaging and gives us breaks throughout the lecture.

Ms Armstrong is extremely passionate and knowledgeable about her subject. Unfortunately I wasn't however her 3 hour lectures were still fun because of her personality and style of teaching. The tests were somewhat hard but fair and my main issue is with the sheer amount of reading she assigns (more than my literature class).

The most boring and long lectures of my life. Chelsea is a nice person and passionate about her job which I admire a lot, however, is a tough grader and unclear in some of her guidelines, especially for the final paper. The course content was interesting, yet readings boring and hard to read.

Her lectures were always super engaging and we often got to get let out a little early from our 3 hour class. There were weekly quizzes based on the readings but they were pretty easy if you've read them. She's definitely my favourite prof so far and I'd highly recommend taking a course with her if you get the chance!

Easy class, just quite repetitive from what I'd learnt in high school so I did not apply myself. Her lectures were all recorded and watch on your own time, readings, quizzes and discussion posts. Marking is extremely reasonable. Quizzes are simple, but on a time crunch, 20 questions in 20 mins (only hard part about this course lol).

A great class and very straight forward. There are readings and a quiz every week, and a group discussion happens every 2 weeks, there is also a final essay. Very easy to pass, and TAs are good markers, and provide good feedback.

Lessons include a lot of readings that are very, very long and you will be tested on them along with lecture material. Additionally, the test questions are extremely specific so it is hard to know what to study. Discussion post grading is unclear and complicated and the overall course design is messy. Do not take this course if you can help it!!!
Do not take a course with this prof. Overcomplicates things as simple as a discussion post and grades horribly with no feedback. Quizzes are incredibly specific.

Joyce seems passionate about the topic which is great. BUT the readings are long and bland, the quizzes are way too specific and difficult, lectures provide terrible explanations, the course format is disorganized, and the way everything is presented seems like it is trying to make us fail. DO NOT take this course.

Took INDG 286; most confusing course and prof. Quizzes were 40% of the final and were so specific on the readings. All the lectures were the same. Very unclear what she expected from us. She randomly gave everyone that came to the last class an extra mark. Didn't gain anything from that course

I have to agree with the reviews here. This entire course needs redesigning. The quizzes have too many questions and not enough time to finish. A lot of reading material and quizzed on minor details. Writing submissions received little to no feedback. told to follow a rubric, but not clear on why low grade is given.

The quizzes were horribly designed and almost made it impossible for you to do well. The lectures were dry and I found them to be quite useless. I would have loved to learn about Indigenous cultures but they did it in the worst way possible. 100% recommend to TAKE IT ONLINE; it wasn't great but I would have never survived it in-person.

INDG studies should be a component of all UG degrees and I've completed a few with different instructors, unfortunately this is not a prof I will recommend. I think you will get a better learning experience with a different instructor and hopefully see your quiz results and get fair marking with feedback. Disappointingly, you won't find that here.

Terrible course design, outdated, not tech savvy. Lectures are poorly recorded & old, tell you to focus on content that does not show up on quizzes. How do you learn when you submit papers & assignments but don't find out right & wrong answers on quizzes or get feedback on assignments/term paper? She's either too busy or doesn't care.

Maddie is a super sweet and understanding prof. If you love class discussion and working with others, this class is great because we often discussed our readings in small groups and shared. The topic is also extremely important. We need to know Canada's brutal history.

Professor Dielissen is a real gem. One of the best profs I've had at SFU, she's approachable and willing to help in any way you need. Dielissen made the course content interesting and the assignment criteria/grading is clear. All I can say is she's an amazing prof and I will be taking more courses with her before graduating! :)

The 2 midterms and 1 essay was worth majority of the grade. The grading was extremely unclear because my classmates and I followed all the directions and instructions but ended up with a grade that didn't match our work. When I asked for clarifying questions on improving I only got vague answers that were already written in the feedback.

This class was terrible - be warned that the quizzes are worth A LOT. Each quiz is 10%*5=50% of ur grade. And they are VERY hard. Do urself a favor and prep like crazy with ur friends beforehand. Otherwise discussions and assignments are super easy. If your smart about the quizzes and do well, easy A+. Genuinely no idea why the quizzes r like this.

great prof! she handled the topics well and made sure we really understood them. super caring and knowledgeable. had 2 tests that were really easy if you studied (all material from the class with no surprises). attendance is mandatory as there are discussions worth participation points. as an Indigenous student myself I really enjoyed the class!
Great class, everything we learned was on the tests, so no surprises. Excellent slide decks, and I actually learned. Would recommend

I had my first Indigenous Studies class with Dr. Shield. She's such a sweetheart and demonstrated her knowledge in the topics well. I was really interested in the course. We had to be tested by two quizzes, where are like exams. Review the slides enough to know what you write. Participation is group work and easy to earn.

meh being my first indg course. attendance is mandatory if you miss class you only got slides to read cuz lecs aren't recorded. didn't really like the class bc everything was in-person even the "quizzes" prof also didn't do much to prepare us aside from telling us to look over slides+emailed us guest speaker slides after the quiz so thats nice lol
To add on with the previous rating , she mark assignments very slow and till now I still NEVER GET TO KNOW MY MID TERM GRADES!!!!!!!!!!!

This course is very easy but she NEVER answers to my emails!!!! I once sent her an email regarding the grades of final paper, she didnt answer to my gmail , few days later she released the final grade ;-; BRUH

Didnt study a thing for her class coz all exams were online and i just got A+ in all exams thru chatgpt. Her grading is okay ish but gives u chances to improve ur grade. Lectures can be pretty boring but its very easy to score A in this class if ur smart with technology. Overall she is nice
From Kwantlen-- She never answers emails, never posts lectures, and assigns 3 10-20 pages of reading. I'm sure she's a lovely lady, but I found this course to be unfulfilling.

does not reply to emails AT ALL, forgets to add questions to quizzes, does not mark on time, assigns several lengthy readings instead of recording lectures, does not clarify anything-- we've had to resort to talking to the dean about her. probably the most absent teacher i've ever had in the last three years.
She is a really good teacher!

I took synchronous class so there was attendance. Treena was very understanding and flexible (just talk to her). The material was fine (basically Indigenous history). The workload was not too bad (like one short essay per week). I was off the track for a while but she helped me a lot so I got A-.

At first, Booty Munching an intimidating major to take on. When I walked into Stans class for the first time and saw his cosmic bulge, I knew I was in the right place. From a young age I dreamed of moments like that. It's weird rly, when i stared at it, It stared back. Then I got a whiff of the crust. Oh did it ever bring me back to the goodolddays

I was initially hesitant to take this course with Dr. Sessional, but I am so glad I did! Lectures are super entertaining and engaging. He marked all of our assignments himself, but he graded easily. This class was definitely a gpa booster. I will never regret taking this class with the one and only, Professor Stan Sessional.
baddie with a sassy prof

Prof Sessional Just might be The G.O.A.T! He is Truly Caring and Is Very patient when It comes To Teaching. He Answers every Question and Fosters Great discussion During Lectures! The entire Class Learned a Lot and we Even Had a Pizza Party at the End! I like Scratching my Gooch and Sniffing my Fingers! Take Stans Course; You won't Regret it!

But in the silence of the cafeteria, I hear a laugh and it was Stan. He understood all my references and we became fast friends.  Our shared humor effortlessly connected our worlds. We spent hours talking about everything. In a place where I felt invisible, Stan made me feel seen, and for the first time in a long while, I wasn't alone.

had to make them laugh quickly or I would blow my chance with them. I made the widest grin I could possibly make (Trying to mimick the "When the imposter is sus" meme) and I said "When the imposter is sus" I then tried to make a face that resembled the "Flushed" emoji (as part of the meme) however I don't think any of them understood the reference

They were all dead silent (honestly they just didn't know how fun of a game it was). I slapped the middle of our table (to mimick the "emergency meeting" feature in Among Us), and I screamed "EMERGENCY MEETING!!!" Again, none of them laughed. One of them looked at me and said "Can you please sit somewhere else?" At this point, I realized that I

I noticed that one of the kids (who happened to be wearing black) was sitting above a vent. I yelled "Black is sus! He's on top of a vent!!" Everyone at the table looked up at me, but no one laughed (they probably didn't get the reference). I wanted to get them familiar with it, so I asked "Do to guys wanna play some Among Us?"

So yesterday at lunch, I was about to eat with my friends from band when I realized that there was an empty seat at a table with some of the popular kids. I decided to take advantage of this opportunity to troll them, and perhaps befriend some of them. I sat at their table, and right as I sat down,

I'm not typically one to glaze, but Stan is a revelation! Stan's humor is a rare gem, polished to perfection with each lecture. His ability to craft memes that resonate, entertain, and occasionally make me snort-laugh is a gift. Stan's dedication to the art form is inspiring, and his students are the lucky recipients of his comedic brilliance.
The greatest professor of all time. Stan's course changed my life.
Favoritee prof at SFU
best prof ever
LOVE THIS GUY!

Love this guy. I have never taken a better class than with Stan. I will be naming my firstborn after him. There is no Stanley Cup but the Stan Sessional Cup. I sing the praises of this glorious man from the rooftops of the galaxy.

The class was conducted in French, but don't worry, you can complete papers and tests in English, which makes everything feel much more manageable. The lectures were quite interesting, and Sarah graded very fairly. I definitely recommend this class to anyone who speaks French!
Dr. Henzi is a fair marker and very accommodating. There are multiple weekly readings, but most are short.

Lectures hold an overwhelming amount of content and her voice makes you want to fall asleep. But overall she's so caring and knowledgeable in her field, often bringing in guest speakers. The exams are incredibly fair too.

This class was taken in french. Sarah is a very knowledgeable and kind person who is passionate about her students and the content she teaches. She brought wonderful guest speakers almost every week and never put anything on the test that was not in her slides. The downside is she was low energy and I had a hard time staying awake in her lectures.

This class was boring and a waste of time and money. Throughout the entire course, only 1 Indigenous member spoke to our class, and it was through Zoom. I also had to make up and write agendas that align with Indigenous values and opinions (rather than my own) in discussion posts and assignments, fearing that I would lose marks.

Amazing prof. Although the lectures are kind of boring, you can tell he's very passionate about the topic. Lectures and readings are easy to follow and he's very open in case you need help. TAs are also very helpful and give very good feedback. Most of your grade comes from the quizzes and final paper. Overall great professor would take again.

His grading feels overly harsh. Even when I follow the rubric closely, it seems nearly impossible to earn a strong grade on discussion posts. On top of that, his feedback often comes across as condescending, which is really discouraging.

I had Bryan for INDG101, and he was great! I'm not sure where the bad reviews are coming from. In my opinion, Bryan is very knowledgeable and eager to help his students understand, providing them with lots of information about assignments and quizzes. If you do what is required of the course and ask questions when confused, you'll get a high grade.

Bryan does not seem to care about his students' learning. He's an unnecessarily harsh grader, focusing more on tiny details than concepts and course material. When communicating with him, his tone is dismissive and condescending. Before taking the course, multiple friends said they felt like Bryan "hated" them. I feel the same now. Stay away.

Took INDG201W online.  Avoid this class with Bryan if you can.  Class is (pretty much entirely) self-taught, the criteria are ambiguous, and the grading was procrastinated until the final weeks.  An outrageous amount of reading and writing (revising) is required.  Poorly organized and sloppy class.

(online class) Everything we covered I could have easily found on YouTube for free. We discussed many important topics at surface level there was no critical thinking needed. Initially I was looking forward to the discussion posts, but I felt strongly discouraged by the professors feedback. His recorded lectures aren't even him speaking

This course material was very important, but they were ridiculous about APA formatting.  Everything is marked very strictly for formatting.  Even if your work does exactly as required from the rubric, marks are deducted for style of writing.  They should be focused on if the student understood the material and message of this course.

The overall course material was important, but the way it was taught didn't always match the values we were told to uphold (like compassion and relational accountability). Feedback was often vague, and at times felt passive aggressive unnecessarily critical.

Professor is known to be a very tough grader. Despite my best efforts, I've struggled to get 50% on discussion posts. One week, his feedback was that I didn't stay on topic, the next week he said I needed to make more personal connections. Review criteria every time I submit plus, yet I'm still unclear on what exactly he's looking for

Good reviews are clearly from Bryan. You can tell from the stark difference in the reviews: either extremely positive or negative. Brian is obsessed with control. I want to steer people clear of this guy. I once commented that we should have more First Nations teaching indigenous courses, and that pissed him off. Just to give you an idea of him

All online, impossible to get a hold of. Bit of an ass when it comes to grading. Unclear instructions and expects you to know exactly what he wants. Would never take again even tho I did alright. Caused sooooooo much stress.
I did ING201 online. Lots of weekly readings, unclear expectations for group discussions, took too long to reply to public discussion board, HARD marker, and lowkey condescending.
Professional, funny, concise, clear, and deeply respectful of the course material.
marked way too hard for a first year, intro course
Super boring snoozefest! Avoid at all costs. The class was generally easy, but it did not have to be 3 hours long. Plus the TA's suck!

Stuck in a farcical course with a prof obsessed with control. Group work is a myth, lectures a snoozefest filled with documentaries. Attendance "optional," yet vital quizzes, worth 45% of our grade, force our hand. It's manipulation, not education. A complete mockery of learning. Disgraceful.

Prof Myles is so easy! He literally does not give any exams and everything is so easy, definitely a gpa booster. I do think he is not the greatest prof though honestly, he does not really seem too interested in teaching and the format of his class was not engaging at all.
Good professor. Make sure you read the feedback as this helped me receive a better grade. Readings aren't to long except they can get boring sometimes. Really easy to talk to.
